title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski hit a walk-off single in the 10th inning, leading the Braves to a 3-2 win over the Red Sox.
Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early gave up two solo home runs but recorded six strikeouts in five innings of work.
The Red Sox managed seven hits but left seven men on base, with a 1-for-3 performance in scoring opportunities.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots' reunion with Stefon Diggs is unlikely due to impending A.J. Brown trade.
Diggs, who had a 1,000-yard season with Patriots, could command around $10 million annually.
Patriots face salary cap constraints with Brown's $32 million AAV and Romeo Doubs' $68 million contract.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, was last seen Tuesday near 965 Blue Hill Ave in Dorchester, reported missing Wednesday.
Described as a Black male, 5'8", thin build, LaGrant-Gomes is known to frequent Columbia Road and Downtown Crossing.
Boston Police request information on his whereabouts; contact District B-3 detectives at 617-343-4712 or CrimeStoppers anonymously.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, is accused of shooting his girlfriend's mother and another man on Mother's Day in Dorchester.
The mother, 42, suffered life-threatening injuries; the second victim, also 42, had non-life-threatening injuries.
Broomes faces multiple charges, including assault with intent to murder; he was held without bail pending a hearing.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Five former Red Sox starting pitchers have a combined ERA of 2.74 in 39 starts for other teams in 2026.
Dominic Smith, a former Red Sox, is a breakout star in 2026, batting over .350 as of May 15.
Former Red Sox player Kyle Schwarber leads MLB in home runs with 18, including two against Boston this week.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roman Anthony has missed nine games since being placed on the injured list on May 7 with a wrist sprain.
Anthony shed his wrist brace and participated in throwing activities, showing progress in his recovery.
The Red Sox hope to have Anthony resume batting soon, depending on his recovery progress and response to treatment.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Jeep crashed into the BrightPath daycare in Norwood on Friday, causing no injuries but moderate structural damage.
The driver mistakenly accelerated while dropping off a passenger, sending the vehicle into the building's glass entryway.
Norwood's building inspector closed the daycare for further structural evaluation; the incident is under investigation.

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict a 10-7 season record for the New England Patriots in 2026 after last year's 14-3.
Patriots face a more challenging 2026 schedule, including Seattle, Denver, and Chicago, impacting win predictions.
Some experts offer differing predictions, with Sports Illustrated predicting 9-8 and Pats Pulpit 12-5 for 2026.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Marty, Life is Short' is a Martin Short documentary now available on Netflix.
'Mars Attacks!' is streaming for free on YouTube and Plex, featuring Martin Short.
'Tucci in Italy' Season 2 explores Italian cuisine, available on Disney+ and Hulu.

title: Mayor Wu remains firm as councilors consider rejecting her budget
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Wu's $4.9 billion budget proposal faces possible rejection by Boston City Council due to spending cuts.
Councilors consider rejecting the budget to prompt resubmission with increased spending before June 10 deadline.
Wu warns against increasing budget, citing potential fiscal irresponsibility and a $50 million current budget deficit.
